rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes for port-level contracts, and in implementation scopes for bindings after subcomponents and connections.
Use visible feature names and concrete bounds when defining range assumptions and guarantees in component type contracts.
generic_rules: Ensure that AGREE annex blocks are placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for structural contracts, implementations for behavioral bindings.
Define value range constraints using visible identifiers and explicit bounds in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use boolean expressions to define range conditions in AGREE: eq variable : bool = value >= min and value <= max;
Annotate AGREE annex blocks with proper system or component scope identifiers.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level assumptions or guarantees in component type scopes unless they are abstracted through variables.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts without proper variable representation.
